era_context: The late Archaic period in Greece; the Neo-Babylonian Empire under Nabopolassar in its consolidation phase; the Spring and Autumn period in China; the Iron Age in the Mediterranean. This year falls within a period of significant political reorganization in the Near East and the early development of Greek city-state culture.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: For the year 618 BCE specifically, no major events are reliably documented in surviving sources. The historical record for this year is essentially blank in all known traditions. This reflects both the genuine scarcity of written records from this period and the fact that ancient historians and chroniclers did not record events year-by-year with modern precision. The broader era (early 6th century BCE) is better documented, but individual years within this decade are largely undifferentiated in the sources.
